ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general paratha entry; no specific onion-stuffed paratha term exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific paratha or pyaaz paratha code. Unleavened bread category used as closest parent.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code exists for pyaaz paratha or paratha flatbread.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard pyaaz paratha weighs approximately 100 g and is typically served as a single flatbread alongside yoghurt, pickle, or curry.

nutrient_source.title: Paratha, onion (pyaaz paratha) — ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods
nutrient_source.organization: Indian Council of Medical Research – National Institute of Nutrition (ICMR-NIN)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods (2017 edition) reports values for plain paratha and onion-stuffed paratha variants. Values for pyaaz paratha are drawn from the onion paratha entry and cross-referenced with USDA SR Legacy whole-wheat flatbread data to confirm plausibility. Per-100g values represent a cooked, pan-fried onion paratha made with whole wheat atta, onion, green chilli, coriander, and a small amount of ghee.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of Indian flatbreads — chapati and paratha analogues
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for pyaaz paratha exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 52 is estimated from the closest analogous food: whole-wheat chapati / paratha (GI approximately 52, low category) as listed in the University of Sydney GI Database and corroborated by Atkinson et al. (2008) International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load, Diabetes Care. The addition of onion filling does not materially raise GI and may modestly lower it due to fructooligosaccharide content. Glycemic load calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (52 × 43.5 × 1.0) / 100 = 22.6 for a 100 g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content approximately 310 mg per 100 g
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit pyaaz paratha to one serving and avoid high-sodium accompaniments such as pickles or salted butter, as the combined sodium load can approach or exceed 600 mg per meal.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index (GI 52) and significant carbohydrate load (43.5 g per 100 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Persons with Type 2 Diabetes should consume pyaaz paratha in controlled portions (one paratha) paired with a high-fibre vegetable side or low-fat yoghurt to blunt the postprandial glucose response, given the glycemic load of approximately 22.6 per serving.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from ghee (approximately 3.1 g per 100 g) and moderate sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For individuals with cardiovascular disease, pyaaz paratha prepared with ghee should be consumed in moderation; substituting ghee with a small amount of canola or olive oil can reduce saturated fat intake and improve the overall fatty acid profile.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pyaaz paratha is a traditional North Indian unleavened flatbread made from whole wheat flour (atta) stuffed or layered with a spiced onion filling comprising finely chopped raw onion, green chilli, fresh coriander leaves, cumin seeds, and salt. The dough is rolled thin, filled, folded, and then cooked on a hot tawa (griddle) with ghee or oil until golden-brown and slightly crisp on the outside while remaining soft and layered inside. It is a staple breakfast and lunch item across Punjab, Haryana, Uttar Pradesh, and Rajasthan, and is widely served in Indian restaurants globally including Singapore. Nutritionally, pyaaz paratha provides approximately 297 kcal per 100 g, with 7.8 g protein, 10.2 g fat, and 43.5 g carbohydrate. The whole wheat base contributes 3.8 g dietary fibre per 100 g, supporting satiety and digestive health. The glycemic index is estimated at 52 (low category), making it a moderate-GI staple relative to refined wheat products. Sodium content of approximately 310 mg per 100 g warrants attention for hypertensive individuals. The onion filling provides small amounts of vitamin C, quercetin, and fructooligosaccharides. Clinically, portion control is important for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or cardiovascular disease due to the combined carbohydrate and saturated fat load from ghee.
common_user_queries: How many calories in onion paratha?
Is onion paratha good for diabetics?
What is the GI of paratha?
Can I eat paratha with high blood pressure?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods (2017) onion paratha entry and cross-referenced with USDA SR Legacy whole-wheat flatbread data. No exact pyaaz paratha entry exists in a single approved source; values represent a well-supported adaptation. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database whole-wheat paratha/chapati data.
notes: vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 (confirmed zero) as pyaaz paratha is a plant-based flatbread containing no animal-derived ingredients that contribute B12; ghee contributes negligible B12 at typical usage levels, confirmed as effectively zero per ICMR-NIN data. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the ICMR-NIN source database for this food category. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the ICMR-NIN source database for this food category. GI of 52 is an analogous estimate based on whole-wheat paratha/chapati from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for pyaaz paratha was located. Sodium value of 310 mg per 100 g reflects home-style preparation with moderate salt; restaurant versions served in Singapore may contain higher sodium. The field dataed (Dugout - Circular Road, field survey data) serves this as a menu item at SGD 8.00; nutrient values are based on standard recipe composition, not restaurant-specific analysis.

improvement_actions_taken: Added mandatory multilingual local_names for Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi as required for Indian cuisine served in Singapore context.
Populated full per_100g nutrient profile from ICMR-NIN onion paratha data cross-referenced with USDA SR Legacy.
Set GI to 52 (analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database whole-wheat paratha/chapati) with gi_category 'low' per ≤55 threshold rule.
Documented vitamin_b12_mcg=0.0 as confirmed zero with justification phrase in notes.
Documented vitamin_d_mcg=-1 and selenium_mcg=-1 sentinel values with source-not-reported justification in notes.
Ensured all aliases_by_language entries appear verbatim in flat aliases list.
Used match_type values only from allowed set: exact, closest_parent, none.
Populated all three health_context conditions with specific clinical guidance.
